rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Motion Of Defendant/"Respondent" Minnesota Lawyers Mutual Insurance Company To Dismiss Or Stay Action On Forum Non Conveniens Grounds [Code Civ. Proc. §418.10(A)(2)]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 15, 2018, Line 11, RESPONDENT MINNESOTA LAWYERS MUTUAL INSURANCE COMPANY'S Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Action On Forum Non Conveniens Grounds [Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 418.10(A)(2)]. Defendant/Respondent Minnesota Lawyers Mutual Insurance Company's ("Minnesota") motion to stay or dismiss action based on forum non conveniens is GRANTED. The court orders this action STAYED pending resolution of Modell's petition for assumption of jurisdiction over trust presently pending in Maryland. The court grants Minnesota's supplemental request for judicial notice. Berman is a California resident, but Minnesota has rebutted the presumption in favor of his choice of forum. Maryland is a suitable alternative forum, all of the interested parties are subject to jurisdiction in that forum, and there is no showing that Modell's petition, which is likely to resolve the issues in this case, is time barred. The public and private factors that are set forth in Stangvik v. Shiley, Inc., (1991) 54 Cal.3d 744, 751 weigh in favor of staying this action. Berman's petition is about a Maryland trust and the conduct of a Maryland-based trustee (Modell). The allegations of the petition belie Berman's argument that trustee issues are not involved in this case and the prior actions. (See Petition, pars. 5, 8, 9, 11, 12, 24, 25, 26, 29, and 30.) Berman's claim that California has an interest in this case because the prior suits were brought here rings hollow. The fundamental issue among the parties is the distribution of funds from a Maryland trust. The fact that prior actions were filed in California is secondary. Berman's request for discovery is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for Minnesota is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: QIAN & NEMECEK LLP VS. YUNJI WILLA QIAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Access To And Copying Of Documents Books And Records Qian And Nemecek Llp And For Monetary Issue Evidence And Claim Cause Of Action Preclusion Terminating Sanctions Against Qian And Nemecek Llp And David Nemecek Jr
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 15, 2018, Line 9, DEFENDANT YUNJI QIAN'S Motion To Compel Access To And Copying Of Documents Books And Records Qian And Nemecek Llp And For Monetary Issue Evidence And Claim Cause Of Action Preclusion Terminating Sanctions Against Qian And Nemecek Llp And David Nemecek Jr. Defendant Qian's motion to compel access to and copying of books and records is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. My April 6, 2018 order provided: "the parties are ordered to meet and confer in person regarding the turnover of partnership documents." Only then was Qian authorized to file a motion to compel, if necessary. However, the motion papers indicate that no in-person meet-and-confer has been held. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: QIAN & NEMECEK LLP VS. YUNJI WILLA QIAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To First Set Of Requests For Production Of Documents And First Set Of Special Interrogatories To Defendant And Crosscomplainant Yunji Willa Qian And Request For Sanctions Against Defendant Qian And Her Counsel In The Amount Of $6,810
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 15, 2018, Line 9, PLAINTIFF QIAN & NEMECEK LLP'S Motion To Compel Responses To First Set Of Requests For Production Of Documents And First Set Of Special Interrogatories To Defendant And Cross-complainant Yunji Willa Qian And Request For Sanctions Against Defendant Qian And Her Counsel In The Amount Of $6,810. Plaintiff's motion to compel responses to the first sets of document requests and special interrogatories to defendant Yunji Qian is GRANTED. Qian is to provide responses to all at-issue interrogatories without objection, and produce all responsive documents, within 20 days of this order's entry. Qian's objections were not justifiable, so $3,000 in sanctions are awarded against her. (CCP 2023.010(e), 2030.300(d), 2031.300(c).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: QIAN & NEMECEK LLP VS. YUNJI WILLA QIAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance With Subpoena Issued To Hall, Yee & Associates And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $5,810
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 15, 2018, Line 9, PLAINTIFF QIAN & NEMECEK LLP'S Motion To Compel Compliance With Subpoena Issued To Hall, Yee & Associates And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $5,810. Plaintiff's motion to compel compliance with a subpoena issued to Hall, Yee & Associates is GRANTED. Hall, Yee is to fully comply with the subpoena within 10 days of this order's entry. Because Hall, Yee failed to comply in bad faith and without substantial justification for months, plaintiff's expenses are awarded from Hall, Yee in the amount of $3,000. (CCP 1987.2.) rulings.Case Title: LOUIS COLATRIANO VS. GATE GOURMET, INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 15, 2018, Line 5, DEFENDANTS GATE GOURMET, INC., DOLORES MEJIA, AND BRETT APPLEBERG'S Motion To Transfer Venue. Defendants' motion for a mandatory change of venue is DENIED. The motion is untimely because the defendants already answered. (See CCP 396b(a); Easton v. Sup. Crt. (1970) 12 Cal.App.3d 243, 246.) Government Code 12965 is not a transfer-of-venue statute. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER GASPAR VS. D.P.P.M. INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Documents Pursuant To Deposition Subpoena For Business Records Served Upon Third Party Fidelity National Title Company
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 15, 2018, Line 1, DEFENDANT JOSEF HERSH, D.D.P.M., INC. DBA ZEPHYR REAL ESTATE Motion To Compel Documents Pursuant To Deposition Subpoena For Business Records Served Upon Third Party Fidelity National Title Company. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant D.P.P.M., Inc, dba Zepher Real Estate's motion to compel documents production pursuant to a deposition subpoena served upon third party fidelity national title company is granted. The third party objections are overruled and within fifteen (15) days from the date of this order, Fidelity National Title Company will produce to defendant all documents responsive to the deposition requesst for the period September, October and November, 2016. The court will sign the protective order submitted by the moving party to protect against any claim of confidential or trade secret information. If Fidelity seeks to withhold any documents asserting a claim of attorney/client privilege it must prepare and deliver within fifteen (15) days from the date of this order to the moving party a privilege log identifying the documents and the basis for the claim of privilege with sufficient specificity to allow moving party to challenge said claim. Counsel for the moving party will prepare an appropriate order, deliver a copy to counsel for Fidelity for approval as to form and deliver it to the court for signing at the hearing on June 15, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT/RU)
